summary: Tested tax exemptions feature for Products and Sale Types. All backend and frontend tests passed. The feature allows selecting which taxes apply to products and sale types via switches.
passed_tests: TestTaxConfig::test_get_tax_config - Verified ITBIS (18%) and LEY (10%) active taxes exist
TestSaleTypes::test_list_sale_types - Sale types have tax_exemptions field
TestSaleTypes::test_delivery_has_propina_exemption - Delivery is exempt from Propina/LEY tax
TestSaleTypes::test_update_sale_type_tax_exemptions - Update tax exemptions via API works
UI: Settings > Ventas > Tipos de Venta shows exemption badges (1 exención(es) for Delivery)
UI: Sale Type Edit Dialog has 'Impuestos Aplicables' section with switches
UI: Delivery shows ITBIS=Aplica (switch ON), LEY=Exento (switch OFF)
UI: Product Config page has 'Impuestos Aplicables' section with tax toggles
critical_code_review_comments: billing.py lines 137-260: create_bill correctly handles tax exemptions at both sale type and product level
billing.py lines 209-224: Tax calculation skips taxes in sale_type_exemptions and product exemptions
Settings.js lines 2882-2944: Sale Type dialog has proper tax switches with data-testid
ProductConfig.js lines 521-605: Product tax exemptions section with clickable checkboxes
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tax exemptions feature fully implemented. Delivery sale type has tax_exemptions=[f8c88ad9-251d-4e47-a7d4-a49bee457b53] (Propina/LEY 10%). Products support tax_exemptions array field. Frontend shows switches in both Sale Type dialog and Product Config page. Admin PIN is 10000.

features_verified.sale_type_exemption_badge: ✓ Delivery shows '1 exención(es)' badge in Tipos de Venta list
features_verified.sale_type_dialog_switches: ✓ Edit dialog has 'Impuestos Aplicables' with ITBIS/LEY switches
features_verified.billing_engine: ✓ create_bill skips exempt taxes (verified in code review)
